items.title: USDA posts state SNAP waivers restricting purchases of soda and candy, with 2026 rollout dates
items.summary: USDA's Food and Nutrition Service lists approved state SNAP food restriction waivers that can block purchases like soda, energy drinks, candy, and some prepared desserts. The USDA page shows target implementation dates ranging from Jan. 1, 2026 (e.g., Indiana/Iowa) to Oct. 1, 2026 (e.g., Missouri), with Florida listed for Apr. 20, 2026 and Arkansas for Jul. 1, 2026.

items.title: CMS guidance: limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens start Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued a State Health Official letter preparing states to implement a statutory change limiting the ability to claim federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for people who are not U.S. citizens or nationals (or who fall into specific noncitizen categories in statute). CMS says the change begins Oct. 1, 2026, with exceptions including continued matching funds for emergency medical conditions and the option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people.

items.title: Medicaid unwinding tracker: at least 25.198 million disenrolled during renewals (latest compiled data)
items.summary: KFF's Medicaid enrollment and unwinding tracker reports that, as of Sept. 12, 2024, at least 25,198,000 Medicaid enrollees had been disenrolled during the post-continuous-coverage renewal process. The tracker also summarizes that 31% of completed renewals ended in disenrollment in reporting states, while 69% resulted in coverage renewal.

items.title: HUD updates Emergency Housing Voucher and Stability Voucher rules: removes 180-day document delay; PHAs must comply within 60 days
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-05 removes the special allowance letting Emergency Housing Voucher (EHV) and Stability Voucher (SV) households provide Social Security number and eligible immigration documentation up to 180 days after admission. HUD directs PHAs to follow the standard SSN and eligible-status verification rules (24 CFR 5.210-5.218 and 24 CFR 5.508) and says PHAs must be in compliance within 60 days of the notice.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: Federal Register: HUD proposes optional work requirements and term limits for public housing and vouchers; comments due May 1, 2026
items.summary: A HUD proposed rule would let eligible Public Housing Agencies adopt optional work requirements (up to 40 hours/week of work activities) and optional term limits (minimum 2 years) for non-elderly, non-disabled households in public housing and several voucher programs. The Federal Register notice lists May 1, 2026 as the public comment deadline.

items.title: Federal Register: HHS proposes reallotment of $3.18M in prior-year LIHEAP funds to FY2026 recipients; comments due Apr. 30, 2026
items.summary: HHS/ACF published a notice proposing to reallocate $3,181,108.07 for FY2026 LIHEAP recipients, including $2,697,089 in FY2025 LIHEAP block grant funds and $484,019.07 in Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) funds. The notice sets an April 30, 2026 deadline for public comments.

items.title: California WIC: expanded package sizes and new food options show up in the WIC app in mid-April
items.summary: California WIC says many whole-grain items are now allowed in packages ranging from 1248 ounces (instead of only 16 ounces), with additional package-size options for items like dry beans and frozen/canned vegetables. The page says new foods and sizes appear in the California WIC App beginning in mid-April.

items.title: IRS: most early EITC/Additional CTC refunds available by March 2, 2026; refund tracker updated by Feb. 21
items.summary: The IRS says most early filers claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) or Additional Child Tax Credit who choose direct deposit should see refunds in bank accounts or on debit cards by March 2, 2026 (if there are no issues with the return). The IRS also says its Where's My Refund? tool will be updated with projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: FAFSA deadline reminder: 202526 FAFSA due June 30, 2026 (federal deadline)
items.summary: USA.gov lists June 30, 2026 as the federal deadline to submit the FAFSA for the 202526 school year. Schools and states may have earlier priority deadlines, but June 30 is the federal cutoff.

items.title: SSA: 2026 COLA is 2.8% and the taxable wage base rises to $184,500
items.summary: Social Security says benefits (including SSI) increase 2.8% for 2026, with Social Security beneficiary increases starting in January 2026 and SSI increases beginning Dec. 31, 2025. SSA also lists a 2026 taxable maximum of $184,500 and an annual earnings limit of $24,480 for workers under full retirement age.

items.title: SSA Red Book: 2026 disability work thresholds updated (SGA $1,690; Trial Work Period $1,210)
items.summary: SSA's Red Book lists 2026 Substantial Gainful Activity (SGA) as $1,690/month for non-blind disability and $2,830/month for blind disability, and a Trial Work Period amount of $1,210/month. The same SSA page lists the 2026 SSI Federal Benefit Rate as $994/month for an individual and $1,491/month for an eligible couple.
